zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Resprin於大約1406年出生在英國可瓦爾聖 文諾福時，她的父親Richard Resprin28歲，母親Jane Kelly22歲。她於大約1430年與Walter Carminow在英國英格蘭可瓦爾聖 文諾福結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1490年在英國英格蘭可瓦爾聖 文諾福享年85歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Resprin于大约1406年出生在英国可瓦爾聖 文諾福时，她的父亲Richard Resprin28岁，母亲Jane Kelly22岁。她于大约1430年与Walter Carminow在英国英格兰可瓦爾聖 文諾福结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1490年在英国英格兰可瓦爾聖 文諾福享年85岁。

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Resprin was born about 1406, in St Winnow, Cornwall, England, her father, Richard Resprin, was 28 and her mother, Jane Kelly, was 22. She married Walter Carminow about 1430, in St Winnow, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1490, in St Winnow, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 85.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Resprin est née environ 1406, à St Winnow, Cornouailles, Angleterre, son père, Richard Resprin, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Jane Kelly, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Walter Carminow environ 1430, à St Winnow, Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1490, à St Winnow, Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 85 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Resprinが約1406年にイギリスコルンワルスト ウィノーで生まれた時，父Richard Resprinは28歳，母Jane Kellyは22歳であった。約1430年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルスト ウィノーでWalter Carminowと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1490年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルスト ウィノーで85歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Resprin(이)가 대략 1406년 영국 코른월 스트 윈에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Resprin(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane Kelly(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1430년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 스트 윈에서 Walter Carminow(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1490년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 스트 윈에서 85세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Resprin родилась около 1406 г. в местности ст винноу, корнвалл, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Resprin было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Kelly, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Walter Carminow около 1430 г. в местности ст винноу, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1490 г. в местности ст винноу, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 85 лет.
